bullets: • Regularization is a technique used to prevent overfitting in machine learning models by adding a penalty term to the cost function that discourages large parameter values.
• The penalty term is typically added to the cost function as a squared term of the parameters, which encourages the model to minimize the magnitude of the parameters.
• The value of the penalty term is controlled by a regularization parameter, often denoted as lambda, which needs to be chosen by the user.

• The choice of lambda balances the trade-off between minimizing the mean squared error and keeping the parameters small, and different values of lambda can lead to different models with varying levels of complexity.

• The goal of regularization is to find a model that generalizes well to new, unseen data, rather than simply fitting the training data perfectly, and to avoid overfitting by reducing the impact of noise and irrelevant features.
raw:
• Regularization is a technique used to prevent overfitting in machine learning models by adding a penalty term to the cost function that discourages large parameter values.
• The penalty term is typically added to the cost function as a squared term of the parameters, which encourages the model to minimize the magnitude of the parameters.
• The value of the penalty term is controlled by a regularization parameter, often denoted as lambda, which needs to be chosen by the user.
• The choice of lambda balances the trade-off between minimizing the mean squared error and keeping the parameters small, and different values of lambda can lead to different models with varying levels of complexity.
• The goal of regularization is to find a model that generalizes well to new, unseen data, rather than simply fitting the training data perfectly, and to avoid overfitting by reducing the impact of noise and irrelevant features.
